title: Elastic (ESTC) Q1 Earnings and Revenues Beat Estimates
text: Elastic delivered adjusted quarterly earnings of $0.60 per share, surpassing analyst expectations of $0.42. This represents a significant 42.86% earnings surprise and demonstrates the company's consistent track record of beating consensus estimates for four consecutive quarters. Revenue Results: The software company reported quarterly revenues of $415.29 million, exceeding the consensus estimate by 4.64%. This reflects year-over-year growth from the prior year's $347.42 million in sales. Year-to-Date Performance: Despite strong quarterly results, Elastic shares have lost about 15.9% since the beginning of the year versus the S&P 500's gain of 10.2%. Stock Outlook: Analysts assigned Elastic a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) rating, suggesting shares are expected to perform in line with the market in the near future. The company's Internet-Software industry ranks in the top 33% of sectors, which could support positive performance ahead. Earnings Consistency: Over the last four quarters, the company has surpassed consensus EPS estimates four times, indicating a pattern of beating expectations. The company has similarly topped revenue estimates four times over the same period. Forward Guidance: Market consensus anticipates next quarter earnings of $0.58 per share on $410.61 million in revenues, with full-year fiscal estimates at $2.28 per share on $1.67 billion in revenues.
